summary.intent: Implement GitHub issue #252: add a framework-agnostic Combobox core component to `ars-components`, including the machine, API attr methods, event helpers, snapshot tests, spec-conformance tests, and proptest invariants, following the project's TDD + post-implementation audit workflow.
summary.outcome: All implementation tasks completed: `selection::combobox` module created with zero missed mutants (281 tested), spec-conformance and all verification gates passed, spec drift fixes landed, commit created and PR opened targeting `main` with `Closes #252`.

repo: `ComponentPart` derive already includes standard traits (Clone, PartialEq, etc.) — adding them manually causes compile errors.
Selection sets in `ars-collections` are `#[non_exhaustive]`, so match arms need wildcard fallbacks.
`type Effect = NoEffect` is the agnostic-component pattern; closures must not be stored in public enums like `FilterMode`.
Project board state is managed via `gh project item-edit --id <PVTI_…> --project-id <PVT_…> --field-id <PVTSSF_…> --single-select-option-id <…>`.

`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` must be run as `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject -p ars-components --features i18n --lib`; the positional test-filter form (`selection::combobox`) is not supported by the installed cargo-insta.
`cargo xci --profile fast` is the mandatory CI gate before any commit/push; it runs fmt, check, clippy, nextest, adapter tests, spec snippet tests, and snapshot-count checks.
Proptest invariants for new selection components follow the existing pattern of being added but marked `#[ignore]`.
Post-implementation audit is a first-class step and includes spec-drift review, two 'anything else?' rounds, llvm-cov coverage, and `cargo mutants` mutation testing against the new file.

code.finding: Test helper function named `service()` collided with local variables in the expanded test suite; renamed to `make_service()` to resolve shadowing.
`Part::Empty` is the correct public variant with data-part string `"empty"` (spec anatomy table said `EmptyState` but spec code blocks said `Empty`; code blocks win).
`empty_attrs` must emit `role="none"` (not `role="status"`) because the Combobox already has a dedicated `LiveRegion` part.
Audit found spec still described core-owned async/debounced effects and a closure-carrying `FilterMode::Custom`; both were corrected to reflect the closure-free agnostic-core contract.
`CompositionEnd` event signature was updated from `CompositionEnd` to `CompositionEnd(String)` to carry the final composed value.

workflow: Mutation testing with `cargo mutants -p ars-components -f <single-file>` takes ~2 hours per pass on this machine; plan for it when scheduling audit time.
Running a concurrent `cargo mutants` job (from a different process) significantly slows each pass — 281 mutants went from ~50 min to ~2 h.
TDD red-first discipline paid off: the failing spec-conformance test before any production code proved the public surface was missing, not just broken.
First mutation pass had 102 missed mutants; a second targeted pass after adding branch-coverage tests cut it to 2; a third pass confirmed zero — three full passes were needed.

Spec drift fixes belong in the same commit as the implementation — the audit discovered that 'FilterMode::Custom' semantics and loading/live-region timing were incorrectly specified; landing those fixes together avoids a mismatched spec+code state in git history.

friction: Mutation testing dominated wall-clock time: three full 2-hour passes (~6 hours total) to reach zero missed mutants.
The polling loop for `cargo mutants` output required hundreds of sequential `write_stdin` calls because the tool streams results incrementally with long silent intervals.
A concurrent `cargo mutants` process from another worktree was already running on the machine, compounding build contention and doubling pass time.
`cargo insta test ... selection::combobox` positional filter form silently failed with the installed cargo-insta version; had to fall back to `--lib` with feature flag.
Initial test harness assumed `Service::new` without `Env`/messages args and `connect` without an explicit send closure — both required adjustment after compile failure.
First `cargo check` pass after module creation surfaced multiple compile errors from `ComponentPart` trait collision, non-exhaustive selection-set match arms, and missing `Key` display impls.
102 missed mutants in the first mutation pass required a large test-writing session covering builder round-trips, lifecycle events, prop sync, keyboard branches, and navigation edge cases.
open_items: Leptos and Dioxus adapter component wrappers for Combobox are explicitly out of scope for #252 and deferred to separate issues.

Combobox proptest invariants (highlight visibility, selection validity after UpdateItems, composition suppression) are compiled but `#[ignore]`d — they should be enabled once the proptest harness is wired for this component.
Codex review loop (Task 12) must still be completed: post `@codex review`, address any threads, and wait for 👍 before closing issue #252.
Existing rustdoc link warnings in `ars-components` outside the combobox module were present before this change and were not fixed.
CI must pass and the PR must be merged before issue #252 can formally be closed on the project board.
